check_results.name: Month
check_results.details: Based on ISO 8601, assumed to be in UTC +8 | YYYY-MM
check_results.description: Based on ISO 8601, assumed to be in UTC +8 | YYYY-MM

check_results.name: Date With Timezone
check_results.details: If not in UTC +8 | YYYY-MM-DD[T]hh:mm+hh:mm e.g. 2015-01-01T12:00+02:00 for UTC +2
check_results.description: If not in UTC +8 | YYYY-MM-DD[T]hh:mm+hh:mm e.g. 2015-01-01T12:00+02:00 for UTC +2
check_results.rule: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM demo_employee_payroll WHERE NOT REGEXP_MATCHES(TRY_CAST(payroll_start_dt AS TEXT), '^[0-9]{4}-(0[1-9]|1[0-2])-(0[1-9]|[12][0-9]|3[01])T([01][0-9]|2[0-3]):[0-5][0-9][+-]([01][0-9]|2[0-3]):[0-5][0-9]$') OR LENGTH(TRY_CAST(payroll_start_dt AS TEXT)) > 22

check_results.name: Date With Timezone

check_results.description: Validates that total_amt equals the sum of employer_cpf_amt and total_amt_employee for consistency
check_results.rule: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM demo_employee_payroll WHERE total_amt <> (employer_cpf_amt + total_amt_employee) OR (NOT total_amt IS NULL AND (employer_cpf_amt IS NULL OR total_amt_employee IS NULL))

check_results.name: phone_number_logical_type_options_pattern_check
check_results.details: Column 'phone_number' must match pattern '^\+[0-9]+$'
check_results.description: Column 'phone_number' must match pattern '^\+[0-9]+$'
check_results.rule: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM "demo_employee_list" WHERE NOT "phone_number" IS NULL AND NOT REGEXP_MATCHES(TRY_CAST("phone_number" AS TEXT), '^\+[0-9]+$')

check_results.name: phone_number_unique_check
check_results.details: Column 'phone_number' must contain unique values
check_results.description: Column 'phone_number' must contain unique values
check_results.rule: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT "phone_number" FROM "demo_employee_list" WHERE NOT "phone_number" IS NULL GROUP BY "phone_number" HAVING COUNT(*) > 1)

check_results.name: PersonName
check_results.details: PersonName may include various name types for UIN/FIN holders including legal names, abbreviated names, Hanyu Pinyin names, alias names, married names, and changed names | X(66)
check_results.description: PersonName may include various name types for UIN/FIN holders including legal names, abbreviated names, Hanyu Pinyin names, alias names, married names, and changed names | X(66)
check_results.rule: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM demo_employee_list WHERE NOT employee_last_name IS NULL AND LENGTH(TRY_CAST(employee_last_name AS TEXT)) > 66

check_results.name: PersonName
check_results.details: PersonName may include various name types for UIN/FIN holders including legal names, abbreviated names, Hanyu Pinyin names, alias names, married names, and changed names | X(66)
check_results.description: PersonName may include various name types for UIN/FIN holders including legal names, abbreviated names, Hanyu Pinyin names, alias names, married names, and changed names | X(66)
check_results.rule: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM demo_employee_list WHERE NOT employee_first_name IS NULL AND LENGTH(TRY_CAST(employee_first_name AS TEXT)) > 66

check_results.name: TelephoneNumber
check_results.details: Based on ITU T E.164 | e.g. 095236
check_results.description: Based on ITU T E.164 | e.g. 095236
check_results.rule: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM demo_employee_list WHERE NOT REGEXP_MATCHES(TRY_CAST(phone_number AS TEXT), '^[0-9]{1,12}$')

check_results.name: employee_id_exists_in_master_list
check_results.details: Referential integrity check: Ensures all employee IDs in the payroll  table exist in the reference employee list.
check_results.description: Referential integrity check: Ensures all employee IDs in the payroll  table exist in the reference employee list.
check_results.rule: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM demo_employee_payroll AS payroll LEFT JOIN demo_employee_list AS ref ON payroll.employee_id = ref.employee_id WHERE ref.employee_id IS NULL

check_results.name: phone_number_exists_in_master_list
check_results.details: Ensures the phone numbers recorded in payroll matches an entry in  the master employee list.
check_results.description: Ensures the phone numbers recorded in payroll matches an entry in  the master employee list.
check_results.rule: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM demo_employee_payroll AS payroll LEFT JOIN demo_employee_list AS ref ON payroll.phone_number = ref.phone_number WHERE NOT payroll.phone_number IS NULL AND ref.phone_number IS NULL

description.purpose: This employee payroll dataset captures monthly compensation and contribution details for workforce financial management. It serves HR, Finance, and Payroll departments by providing a complete record of employee earnings, CPF (Central Provident Fund) contributions from both employer and employee sides, enabling accurate payroll processing and statutory compliance reporting.
description.usage: Use this data to reconcile monthly payroll runs, generate CPF submission reports, and analyze total compensation costs per employee. Query by employee_id and month to retrieve individual payroll records, or aggregate by payroll period (payroll_start_dt to payroll_end_dt) for period-based financial reporting and budgeting analysis.
description.limitations: This is a demonstration dataset with only 3 sample records and should not be used for production reporting. The data appears to be Singapore-specific given the CPF contribution fields, so it may not be applicable to employees in other jurisdictions without CPF requirements.
